news.t: 4 Active ETFs to Ride India's Comeback

holdings.infoBox: GIND is an equity ETF with a total of 125 individual holdings. The top holdings are ICICI Bank stock at 5.88%, HDFC Bank at 3.69%, Reliance Industries at 2.93%, Axis Bank at 2.74%, and Bajaj Finance at 2.50%.

overview.description: The Goldman Sachs India Equity ETF (GIND) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI India IMI index. The fund is an actively managed portfolio of Indian equities of various market capitalizations. The fund seeks capital appreciation GIND was launched on Apr 1, 2025 and is issued by Goldman Sachs.

data.title: 4 Active ETFs to Ride India's Comeback
data.text: India's youthful population is more interested in domestic consumption than citizens in export-focused nations like China and Japan. That should drive growth for local businesses.
